transcript: How are y'all doing? Caffeinated? All right. Um, so, uh, this will be explained. So, uh, really love the opportunity to talk to all of you a little bit about what we've been working on at Helium this year and and things that you should be seeing uh over the coming months. Um, just for the folks in the room who don't know what Helium is, um, we are a network that allows you to deploy coverage and provide connectivity to devices and to people. Um, we really, really are here because we believe that we can rev we can all revolutionize the way we all connect. Um, we started the network back in 2019 with as an IoT network. We uh since have since expanded to a mobile network and, you know, our goals and the things that I'll talk about mostly here is, you know, how we can connect everyone. So, there are a couple of things that we worked on this year that I think are worth talking about. I think the biggest thing um that we've done is really allow anyone to be able to deploy coverage um without having to install net new hardware. So a venue like this arena right here already has Wi-Fi deployed, already has radios around. Um, these should be very easily added to the Helium network, and we are seeing this already in the United States, um, where you can bring your own hotspot. This is what we call our devices. Um, and it's a small software change. 15 minutes uh at a network controller and you can enable instant coverage for anyone that walks in uh the door. And and, you know, we fundamentally believe that when you sort of walk around the world, you shouldn't have to, you know, ask for the Wi-Fi password. You should just be able to connect, and you have everything you need in your pocket already. Um, you have a SIM. That SIM is your identity. That identity should be enough to connect anywhere you go. And and we're already seeing a lot of really great coverage being deployed. These are some of the biggest hotspot deployments that we've had. And, you know, that first one, the Fit Khaki Parrot, um, is actually an airport. It's Baltimore Airport in the United States. And every single passenger when they land or when they arrive at the airport, uh if you're an AT&T subscriber or a Helium Mobile subscriber or some of the other carriers that we work with, you automatically connect. You don't have to go through a captive portal. Um, and and it just works for your cell phone coverage. Uh, the second one here is a is a uh casino in Las Vegas. It's the Fontainebleau. Uh, the third one's a mall in Florida. And these are the kinds of things that we're seeing um is really sort of expanding the network. And and what it's really allowing us to do is connecting 2 million, 2.1 million phones every single day. And these are subscribers of not just Helium Mobile, our native carrier, but, you know, folks like AT&T. Uh, and these folks have no idea that this is enabled by a crypto network. Um, what's incredible about this is it's just providing them great connectivity. They don't need to think about this. And, you know, we're seeing terabytes of data transferred every day. And this is just on a network of 120,000 hotspots. And remember, a hotspot could be an entire arena. The things that we are doing now to try to enable this to expand and and grow even faster is really allowing every single one of these hotspots to immediately serve data on these carriers as soon as they're onboarded. And what this allows us to do, it allows carriers to be able to understand the quality of service anytime you deploy new hardware. And really, if you're a deployer, what you care about is the second thing. It means that you get rewarded faster. Every time data gets transferred on the Helium network, you are paid. Uh, and every time someone's transferring data on your network, you are paid. And this is really what we want to see. We want to see more deployments, not just in the United States, and we'll talk a little bit about where we're going next. Um, uh, but we want to see more and more deployments serving more and more customers. Um, and so I'll take a a quick pause on the on the network side and talk about what we've done on the on the carrier side. We launched our own native carrier, Helium Mobile, and it's the first carrier that offers you a free phone plan that rewards you. One of the things that we did this year is we removed the crypto from the the product, from the front end of the product. For the average consumer, they are uh signing up, they're getting a free phone plan, a $15 a month phone plan or a $30 a month phone plan depending on the features that they're interested in. And they're and if they're paying, they're just paying in fiat. Um, and this is really, really been incredible for us this year. We have over 500,000 signups uh just in the last year. We started in the tens of thousands at the start of the year. And this is not just enabling the sort of individual user, but it's also enabling families, groups, you know, neighbors, cousins, whoever. Um, and all of this is really allowing us to continue to grow. And this is one of the things that I've been telling a lot of founders is how do you be more of a crypto-enabled product and less of a just a crypto product? Because this is how you get to scale. And, you know, we think we're just getting started here. And all of this is powered by HNT. And HNT, we think, is a currency of connectivity. HNT is what deployers get paid when they provide useful coverage. And one of the other things that's happened this year uh in the last three months is that HNT has become net deflationary. That means that there is more HNT being burned every single day than being emitted. Um, these dashboards are from uh our friends at Blockworks. Blockworks launched uh a really, really great uh set of dashboards that, you know, really uh uh, you know, dives into every single bit of data that you could have uh on on the Helium network. And, you know, we're really excited to see that too. Quickly, let me talk about what's coming soon. We're launching in Mexico. Uh, we're in the final technical integration stages here. Um, and we should be seeing this hopefully in the next few weeks to month. And the reason I'm wearing this is not because of the World Cup next year. Uh, but we announced this week that we are bringing Helium to Brazil. There's already 40,000 hotspots deployed that can be enabled for Helium Helium coverage, and we're really excited to work with a partner, Mambo, there uh over the next few months. If you'd like Helium to come to your country, that's what you do right now. helium.com/waitlist. Tell us about uh where you'd like to see us go and uh we'll work on that next. Thank you all.

sources.title: Solana Company Announces Intent to Tokenize HSDT Shares on Superstate’s Opening Bell | Solana Company
sources.snippet:
[Skip to main navigation](https://ir.heliusmedical.com/ir.heliusmedical.com#main-menu)

# Release Details

## Solana Company Announces Intent to Tokenize HSDT Shares on Superstate’s Opening Bell

November 12, 2025 at 8:30 AM EST

[PDF Version](https://ir.heliusmedical.com/node/11936/pdf)

NEWTOWN, Pa., Nov. 12, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Solana Company (NASDAQ: HSDT) (the “Company” or “HSDT”), a publicly listed company that has expanded its business to include a digital asset treasury dedicated to acquiring and holding Solana (SOL), today announced its plans to tokenize shares of its HSDT fund on [Superstate](https://www.globenewswire.com/Tracker?data=WCL2jv2WHVrGZ33XGo1sAeEfAq8FyCXbWQqngjLTMK_TY4QsZ0isV15p3IWP4i7G2cToj70PD5ma-72qy87nqA==)’s Opening Bell platform. The initiative marks a major step forward in the Company’s strategy to modernize access to capital markets through regulated, onchain infrastructure.

Upon launch, shareholders will be able to hold and transfer tokenized representations of HSDT shares on Superstate’s Opening Bell, enabling 24/7 trading, real-time settlement, and enhanced global liquidity. The tokenized HSDT will retain its current regulatory structure and investor protections while gaining the efficiency and accessibility of blockchain technology.

“The tokenization of HSDT through Superstate represents a major step toward realizing the vision of global, around-the-clock capital markets, and we believe the majority of that activity will take place on Solana,” said Cosmo Jiang, General Partner at Pantera Capital and Board Director at Solana Company. “Blockchain infrastructure will underpin the next evolution of traditional finance, and Solana Company’s leadership in bringing tokenized assets to market exemplifies the kind of forward thinking that will define this new era.”

The partnership underscores Solana Company’s commitment to driving innovation in real-world-asset (RWA) tokenization and onchain capital markets.

“Solana Company’s real, SEC-registered shares will be available on Solana, accessible in a crypto wallet 24/7. That’s the seismic change in capital markets we’re leading at Superstate,” said Robert Leshner, Chief Executive Officer and Co-Founder of Superstate. “Today’s announcement is a step forward in the momentum shift bringing public capital markets onchain.”

Opening Bell, launched by Superstate in May 2025, is a regulated onchain issuance platform that allows companies to issue and maintain tokenized equity and fund shares on the Solana blockchain. The platform enables compliant, programmable assets to integrate directly with digital finance ecosystems while preserving institutional-grade controls and transparency.

Pantera Capital, an early investor in Superstate and long-time partner to Solana Company, will support the initiative as part of its broader mission to accelerate institutional adoption of tokenized markets.

The partnership follows Solana Company’s $500 million private investment in public equity (“PIPE”) financing, which [closed](https://www.globenewswire.com/Tracker?data=2d9yAonE9LzHynf69QBqv12fj0eAjpi0mRIJg3EuXvEoPvzjwcWlyi4tTpIxQq_wDN5WqisNvrmxaD1cqPfgynbh9dIqRg0N6iuMnpliIzIkcblE4i6dnTDD65kZbOYtNscPF9A16rlTLKmyugz3iN3d34GZskz0sjO8pn2qBQGJwR4Ib1AoZEdaHXApT-TFFvEcX-wh5gaVLYDNE7FIoCJPc3QQpqk96Hfqt3AhhIWXJlI_cS-pTuxCnzOVJ20X48k_8x0bZteTtq8t80b3lI85JIqu7QPn4R5QzcA3959RnWk4-RKV9IaLfsgyVQEz) in September 2025 and represented one of the largest digital asset treasury fundraisings of the year. The PIPE was led by Pantera Capital and Summer Capital, alongside participation from strategic investors and other institutional partners focused on the Solana ecosystem. The financing underscored broad institutional support for Solana Company’s strategy to expand its Solana-based digital asset treasury (“DAT”) and accelerate the integration of real-world assets into onchain capital markets.

**About Superstate**

Superstate is a financial technology firm reshaping public capital markets

tags.impact_reason: Helium's keynote highlights its expansion and integration with major carriers, providing connectivity to millions of users daily, but its impact on the Solana ecosystem is not yet ecosystem-shifting.

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose a Wi-Fi router as the 'Semantic Anchor' because the story's core is about Helium leveraging existing, everyday hardware through a simple software change to rapidly expand its network. The router represents the physical, decentralized node. For the text, I chose 'NET DEFLATIONARY' because this is a monumental economic shift for the protocol mentioned directly in the standfirst. Placing it on a holographic HUD projected from the device makes it diegetic and aligns with the high-tech, 'Wired' aesthetic.
visual_idea.description: A sleek, minimalist Wi-Fi router sits on a polished concrete shelf inside a modern apartment. Through the window behind it, the vibrant, sprawling city of São Paulo is visible at dusk. The router glows from within, emitting a complex, organic network of cyan and teal light tendrils that creep across the concrete like a bioluminescent mycelial network. A small, elegant holographic display hovers just above the router, its light catching the glass of the window, displaying the clean, sans-serif text 'NET DEFLATIONARY'. The background has a shallow depth of field, focusing on the router while the city lights blur into a beautiful bokeh.

final_prompt.prompt_text: Vertical composition (3:4 aspect ratio) emphasizing height and epic scale, with significant negative space above. A monumental, sleek, minimalist Wi-Fi router sits centered on a polished concrete shelf within a sophisticated, modern apartment, exuding Tropical Modernism architecture. Through the expansive window behind, the vibrant, sprawling city of São Paulo sprawls into the twilight, lights beginning to twinkle. The router emits an internal, profound cyan and teal bioluminescent glow, forming intricate, organic network tendrils that spread across the concrete shelf like a luminous, digital mycelial network, suggesting complex data visualization and subtle particle glow. Just above the router, a small, elegant Glassmorphism holographic display shimmers, its light catching the window glass with exquisite reflections, clearly displaying the clean, sans-serif text 'NET DEFLATIONARY'. The background beyond the router is rendered with a shallow depth of field, sharply focusing on the router and its glowing network, while the city lights outside blur into a beautiful, ethereal bokeh. Ultra-realistic, 8k, cinematic quality, octane render, raytracing, hyper-detailed, volumetric lighting, exquisite reflections, glossy reflections.

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose a Wi-Fi router as the 'Semantic Anchor' because the story's core is about Helium leveraging existing, everyday hardware through a simple software change to rapidly expand its network. The router represents the physical, decentralized node. For the text, I chose 'NET DEFLATIONARY' because this is a monumental economic shift for the protocol mentioned directly in the standfirst. Placing it on a holographic HUD projected from the device makes it diegetic and aligns with the high-tech, 'Wired' aesthetic.
visual_idea.description: A macro, top-down shot focusing on the surface of the white Wi-Fi router. Intricate, glowing cyan filaments of the Helium network erupt from the seams of the plastic casing, branching out in fractal patterns across the surface. The lighting is dramatic, catching the texture of the plastic and the ethereal glow of the data network. The image is tightly cropped to emphasize the transformation of a mundane object into a source of vibrant, decentralized power.

final_prompt.prompt_text: A macro, top-down, square (1:1) shot, emphasizing symmetry and central focus, tightly cropped to fill the frame with rich texture. The subject is the surface of a pristine white Wi-Fi router. Intricate, highly detailed, glowing cyan filaments of the Helium network erupt dramatically from the seams of the plastic casing, branching out in complex fractal patterns across the surface. These bioluminescent filaments possess a glassmorphic quality, with translucent, ethereal glow and subtle internal reflections, intensely illuminating the surrounding textured plastic with vibrant cyan light. The lighting is intensely cinematic and ultra-photorealistic, creating dramatic depth shadows and glossy reflections that accentuate the fine texture of the plastic and the ethereal, data-like glow of the network. The image powerfully emphasizes the transformation of a mundane object into a source of vibrant, decentralized power, rendered with a clean, tropical modernism aesthetic. 8k, octane render, raytracing, hyper-detailed, ultra-photorealistic, studio render, cinematic lighting, dramatic volumetric light, depth of field, sharp focus, glossy reflections.

editorial.headline: Helium Enters Brazil via 40,000-Node Mambo WiFi Partnership
editorial.standfirst: The DePIN protocol pivots to a capital-light expansion model, leveraging existing infrastructure to instantly scale coverage in Latin America. With 100% of carrier revenue now burning HNT, the network has officially turned net deflationary.
key_takeaways: Brazil Expansion: New joint venture with Mambo WiFi activates 40,000 existing access points, targeting 100 million under-connected users.
Deflationary Economics: HNT supply is now net deflationary following the August 2025 halving and a shift to burn 100% of subscriber revenue.
Carrier Validation: Network usage has hit 2.1 million daily unique devices, driven by offload partnerships with Tier 1 carriers like AT&T.
editorial.article_body_markdown:
The era of "build it and they will come" is over for **Helium**; the network is now in the "integrate and monetize" phase. In a keynote at Breakpoint 2025, the team revealed a strategic pivot toward "Bring Your Own Hotspot" (BYOH) software integrations, moving away from a pure hardware-sales model. The headline announcement is a massive partnership with **Mambo WiFi** in Brazil, which will instantly onboard 40,000 existing enterprise access points onto the Helium network. This allows Helium to bypass the capital-intensive phase of shipping miners, instantly creating a nationwide footprint in South America's largest economy.

For institutional investors, the real signal lies in the carrier offload metrics. The network is no longer a sandbox for crypto enthusiasts; it is active infrastructure for Tier 1 carriers. **Abhay Kumar** confirmed that 2.1 million unique phones now connect to Helium daily, many of them **AT&T** subscribers who are unknowingly offloading data onto the decentralized network. High-traffic venues like **Baltimore Airport** (the "Fit Khaki Parrot" node) and the **Fontainebleau Las Vegas** are now live, transferring terabytes of data daily. This proves the network can meet the Service Level Agreements (SLAs) required by major telcos.

Financially, the protocol has reached a critical inflection point. Following the August 2025 halving and a governance change to burn 100% of **Helium Mobile** subscriber revenue, HNT has become net deflationary over the last quarter. With the mobile carrier arm surpassing 500,000 paying subscribers—who pay in fiat, not crypto—the buy-and-burn pressure on the token is now driven by recurring real-world revenue rather than speculative demand.
